items.headline: May 1 stock close
items.diff_narrative: **New: May 1 stock close — S&P 500 7,230.12 (+0.29%) new record, Nasdaq Composite 25,114.44 (+0.89%) first-ever close above 25K; both posted weekly gains; Dow -0.31% to 49,499.27 the lone laggard; AAPL closed +3.28% to $280.25 the morning after the Q2 after-close print; Apple gained ~5% intraday at the open; Apr Mag-7 monthly tape (best month since 2020) anchored on Alphabet +34% / AAPL +5% / META -7.5% / MSFT -3.8%** — Yesterday's report had the Apr 30 close (S&P 7,196.01 / Nasdaq 24,890.79). Today adds the May 1 leg + the Apple after-print rally + the Nasdaq through-25K barrier. The S&P closed back-to-back records and the Nasdaq printed its first-ever close above 25K on the same session AAPL closed at $280.25 — the cleanest single-day cohort confirmation of the iPhone-17 super-cycle thesis.

items.headline: Microsoft Agent 365 GA May 1
items.diff_narrative: **New: Microsoft Agent 365 GA May 1 — $15/user/month, included as part of M365 E7 SKU; three pillars (observe / govern / secure); tools management lets admins centrally view + allow/block Microsoft MCP servers across the tenant; integrates with Entra ID + Purview + Intune + Defender; starting June 2026 Defender adds asset-context mapping per agent (devices, MCP servers, identities, cloud resources); commercial + education tenants can activate from the M365 admin center immediately; GCC + GCC High late 2026, DoD early 2027** — Yesterday's report did not have this. The MCP-server allow/block primitive is the missing enterprise gate that turns the Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named-techniques (MCP server compromise) from a threat-model into an operational control; structurally pairs with Apr 30 Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework 1.0 GA as the vendor-side enforcement substrate.

items.headline: CISA KEV May 1 adds CVE-2026-31431 "Copy Fail" Linux kernel local privilege escalation (CVSS 7.8 but BOD-22-01 mandatory); federal deadline May 15; algif_aead AF_ALG socket + splice() interaction enables a 4-byte write into kernel page cache of any readable file, corrupting in-memory representation of /usr/bin/su to yield root without modifying the on-disk binary; affects virtually every Linux distro from 2017 onward
items.diff_narrative: **New: CISA KEV May 1 adds CVE-2026-31431 "Copy Fail" Linux kernel local privilege escalation (CVSS 7.8 but BOD-22-01 mandatory); federal deadline May 15; algif_aead AF_ALG socket + splice() interaction enables a 4-byte write into kernel page cache of any readable file, corrupting in-memory representation of /usr/bin/su to yield root without modifying the on-disk binary; affects virtually every Linux distro from 2017 onward — Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL 10.1, SUSE 16, Debian, Fedora, Arch; multiple in-the-wild attacks observed against US think tanks, European manufacturing, cloud service providers; attack chain pairs Copy Fail with a separate web-app / VPN-appliance RCE for initial foothold** — Yesterday's report had the Apr 30 KEV add (CVE-2026-41940 cPanel & WHM, deadline May 21). Today adds the May 1 layer — Copy Fail's install base is the largest single-vendor KEV-affected base of 2026 (every Linux production machine on kernel 4.x+).

items.headline: Pentagon May 1 signs classified-network AI deals with 7 companies (SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, NVIDIA, Reflection, Microsoft, AWS)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Pentagon May 1 signs classified-network AI deals with 7 companies (SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, NVIDIA, Reflection, Microsoft, AWS) — Anthropic explicitly excluded after refusing the use-of-Claude-for-"all-lawful-purposes" clause covering autonomous weapons + mass domestic surveillance; DoD designated Anthropic a "supply chain risk" (label historically reserved for foreign-adversary vendors); federal judge in California blocked the Trump-admin freeze last month after Anthropic's suit; Trump CoS Susie Wiles met CEO Dario Amodei Apr 17, Trump said deal "possible"** — Yesterday's report flagged TechCrunch's Apr 28 piece on Google expanding Pentagon AI access. Today is the structural completion — 7 named vendors, Anthropic explicitly out, formal "supply chain risk" label, court-tested freeze precedent. First publicly-disclosed presidential-administration vs frontier-AI-lab autonomous-weapons-use stand-off.

items.headline: State Department May 1 sanctions Iran-China oil trade
items.diff_narrative: **New: State Department May 1 sanctions Iran-China oil trade — designates Qingdao Haiye Oil Terminal (China terminal that imported tens of millions of barrels of sanctioned Iranian crude); Treasury concurrently sanctions 3 Iranian currency exchange houses + IRGC/MODAFL-linked individuals/companies processing billions annually; pairs with Trump's standing Jan 12 25% secondary-tariff threat on Iran trading partners + Apr 8 50% tariff threat on weapons suppliers; Foreign Policy May 1 op-ed argues the USTR Section 301 Iran plan is "neither coherent nor defensible"** — Yesterday's report did not have May 1 enforcement. Today adds the largest single-day Iran package of Q2 2026 + first joint Treasury-State strike targeting Chinese terminal operators directly.

items.headline: Apr Jobs Report does NOT release May 1
items.diff_narrative: **Correction: Apr Jobs Report does NOT release May 1 — actual scheduled release is Friday May 8 at 8:30 AM ET per BLS schedule; yesterday's report incorrectly cited May 1 8:30 AM EDT as the release. The May 1 macro tape this morning was Q2 print absorption + Iran enforcement, not the Apr employment print** — Carry-forward: the Apr employment print remains the first major non-earnings macro tape into the May 15 Chair handoff window, but it lands one week later than yesterday's report stated.

items.headline: Apptronik Apr 28 strategic executive hires from top tech giants accelerating commercialization of Apollo humanoid; expansion follows the Feb 2026 $935M Series A at $5B+ valuation; signal of upcoming new humanoid debut + production scale-up; pairs with Figure 03 production at 1/hour (24x throughput in 120 days) + Figure overnight-fully-autonomous demos + Tesla Optimus Gen 3 low-volume summer 2026
items.diff_narrative: **New: Apptronik Apr 28 strategic executive hires from top tech giants accelerating commercialization of Apollo humanoid; expansion follows the Feb 2026 $935M Series A at $5B+ valuation; signal of upcoming new humanoid debut + production scale-up; pairs with Figure 03 production at 1/hour (24x throughput in 120 days) + Figure overnight-fully-autonomous demos + Tesla Optimus Gen 3 low-volume summer 2026** — Yesterday's report did not have the Apr 28 Apptronik hires. Today adds it as the third-mover signal (Tesla + Figure + Apptronik all converging on commercial deployment in summer/autumn 2026).

items.headline: Cerebras IPO roadshow extends into May 2026 with mid-May Nasdaq listing under ticker CBRS targeted at $22-25B; pre-IPO secondary trading at $26-28B fully diluted; SEC review proceeds smoothly per company; underwriters Morgan Stanley / Citi / Barclays / UBS
items.diff_narrative: **New: Cerebras IPO roadshow extends into May 2026 with mid-May Nasdaq listing under ticker CBRS targeted at $22-25B; pre-IPO secondary trading at $26-28B fully diluted; SEC review proceeds smoothly per company; underwriters Morgan Stanley / Citi / Barclays / UBS** — Carry-forward; today the May 1 record-tape ratification of the AI-capex thesis is the swing factor for IPO-day pricing dynamics. Mid-May window avoids competing with May 8 Apr Jobs print + May 5 AMD print.

items.headline: arxiv 2604.27776 WindowsWorld (process-centric benchmark of autonomous GUI agents in professional cross-application environments); arxiv 2604.24955 BenchGuard (first automated auditing framework for task-oriented execution-based agent benchmarks; identified 12 author-confirmed issues in ScienceAgentBench); arxiv 2604.20801 multi-agent harness for vulnerability discovery (10 zero-day CVE in Google Chrome including CVE-2026-5280 + CVE-2026-6297 use-after-free sandbox-escape pair); arxiv 2604.10352 ClawVM (harness-managed virtual memory for stateful tool-using LLM agents); arxiv 2604.10235 CodeComp (structural KV cache compression for agentic coding); arxiv 2604.25080 CacheFlow (3D-parallel KV cache restoration); arxiv 2604.03143 TokenDance (collective KV cache sharing for multi-agent serving); arxiv 2604.19769 TTKV (Temporal-Tiered KV Cache for long-context inference); preprints.org 202604.0428 Agent Harness for LLM Agents Survey
items.diff_narrative: **New: arxiv 2604.27776 WindowsWorld (process-centric benchmark of autonomous GUI agents in professional cross-application environments); arxiv 2604.24955 BenchGuard (first automated auditing framework for task-oriented execution-based agent benchmarks; identified 12 author-confirmed issues in ScienceAgentBench); arxiv 2604.20801 multi-agent harness for vulnerability discovery (10 zero-day CVE in Google Chrome including CVE-2026-5280 + CVE-2026-6297 use-after-free sandbox-escape pair); arxiv 2604.10352 ClawVM (harness-managed virtual memory for stateful tool-using LLM agents); arxiv 2604.10235 CodeComp (structural KV cache compression for agentic coding); arxiv 2604.25080 CacheFlow (3D-parallel KV cache restoration); arxiv 2604.03143 TokenDance (collective KV cache sharing for multi-agent serving); arxiv 2604.19769 TTKV (Temporal-Tiered KV Cache for long-context inference); preprints.org 202604.0428 Agent Harness for LLM Agents Survey** — Yesterday had the Apr 27 PolyKV + Apr 25 SnapGuard + AgentWatcher + LogJack + Apr 28 SSL agent skill structure wave. Today adds the late-Apr / early-May agent-harness + benchmark + KV-cache wave — BenchGuard + multi-agent harness vulnerability discovery + WindowsWorld are the standouts.

items.headline: AMD Q1 2026 print May 5 after-close
items.diff_narrative: **New: AMD Q1 2026 print May 5 after-close — guidance $9.8B revenue (+/- $300M), midpoint +32% YoY; consensus rev $9.84B; record Q4 2025 Instinct GPU revenue led by MI350; investors watching MI400/Helios 2H26 ramp; DA Davidson upgrades to Buy at $375 PT; conference call 5pm ET / 2pm PT** — Yesterday flagged May 5 AMD; today adds the DA Davidson Buy upgrade + the MI400/Helios specific watch-item.

items.headline: Microsoft Build 2026 confirmed June 2-3 in San Francisco
items.diff_narrative: **New: Microsoft Build 2026 confirmed June 2-3 in San Francisco — Satya Nadella keynote on AI strategy + Azure roadmap + developer tooling; GitHub + Microsoft Foundry leadership presenting; session catalog now live; livestream registration open** — Yesterday did not flag the Build session catalog publication. Today does. Build now sits as the next major AI platform-announcement window after May 1 Agent 365 GA.

items.headline: ICLR 2026 outstanding papers announced Apr 23
items.diff_narrative: **New: ICLR 2026 outstanding papers announced Apr 23 — 5,300+ accepted papers; 225 oral presentations; "LLMs Get Lost In Multi-Turn Conversation" by Laban / Hayashi / Zhou / Neville; other notable papers Common Corpus (ethical LLM pre-training data), Q-RAG (multi-step retrieval via RL-trained embedders), WebDevJudge (LLM-as-a-judge for web dev), SafeDPO (safe direct preference optimization), MedAgentGym (agentic biomedical training)** — Carry-forward into the Apr 30-May 1 research window; the multi-turn-conversation degradation finding is the structural piece that pairs with PolyKV + ClawVM + the broader 2026 agentic-context-management research wave.
